content.problemStatement:
It's finally summer in Chefland! So our chef is looking forward to prepare some of the best "beat-the-heat" dishes to attract more customers. He summons the Wizard of Dessert to help him with one such dish.

 The wizard provides the chef with a sequence of **N** ingredients where the **ith** ingredient has a delish value of **D[i]**. The preparation of the dish takes place in two phases. 

**Phase 1 :** The chef chooses two indices **i** and **j** and adds the ingredients **i, i+1, ..., j** to his dish. He also finds the sum of the delish value in this range i.e **D[i] + D[i+1] + ... + D[j]**.  

**Phase 2 :** The chef chooses two more indices **k** and **l** and adds the ingredients **k, k+1, ..., l** to his dish. He also finds the sum of the delish value in this range i.e **D[k] + D[k+1] + ... + D[l]**. 


Note that **1**  ≤ **i**  ≤ **j** < **k**  ≤ **l** ≤ **N**. 




The total delish value of the dish is determined by the absolute difference between the values obtained in the two phases. Obviously, the chef wants to maximize the total delish value for his dish. So, he hires you to help him.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case starts with an integer N, the number of ingredients. The next line contains N space-separated integers representing the delish values of the ingredients.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print the maximum delish value of the dish that the chef can get.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For the first test case, the chef can choose i = j = 1, k = 2, l = 5. The delish value hence obtained is | (2+3+4+5) - (1) | = 13. For the second test case, the chef can choose i = 1, j = 2, k = 3, l = 4. The delish value hence obtained is | ( (1) + (1) ) - (1 + 1) | = 4.
The chef can choose i = 0, j = 0, k = 1, l = 2. The delish value hence obtained is | (2+3) - (-1) | = 6.
The chef can choose i = 0, j = 0, k = 1, l = 1. The delish value hence obtained is | (1000000000) - (-1000000000) | = 2000000000.
The chef can choose i = 0, j = 0, k = 1, l = 1. The delish value hence obtained is | (1) - (1) | = 0.
The chef can choose i = 0, j = 4, k = 5, l = 9. The delish value hence obtained is | (-5) - (-5) | = 10.

solution.approach: The problem can be solved by using a prefix sum array to efficiently calculate the sum of delish values for any range of ingredients. Then, for each possible range of ingredients in the first phase, we calculate the sum of delish values and iterate over all possible ranges in the second phase to find the maximum absolute difference.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * N^4) per test case, O(T * N^4) total. The time complexity is dominated by the four nested loops.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) — the dominant structure is the prefix sum array.
